annotations.comment:
capa:doc=A dialect with an arbitrary single-character delimiter. A
delimiter that is not exactly one character, or that is the
quote character itself, is a programming error the parser
rejects with `BadDialect` rather than misbehaving silently.

capa:doc=A human-readable, backend-identical rendering of an error.
Positions are 1-based record numbers, the unit an operator
reasons about.
capa:doc=Parse CSV text with the default comma dialect.
capa:doc=Parse CSV text with an explicit dialect.

capa:doc=Parse CSV text and take its first record as the header.
Empty input yields an empty header and no rows.
capa:doc=Parse with an explicit dialect, first record as the header.

capa:doc=Build a header view from an already-parsed Table. The first
record becomes the columns; the rest become the data rows.
capa:doc=The number of data records (header excluded).
capa:doc=The zero-based index of a column, or None if absent.
capa:doc=Whether the header declares a column by this name.

capa:doc=The value of `column` in data record `row_index`
(zero-based, header excluded). Errors with `NoSuchColumn`
when the header has no such column, with `NoSuchRow` when
there is no data record at `row_index`, and with `ShortRow`
when the record exists but has fewer fields than the
column's index.
capa:doc=One lowercase hex character for a nibble in 0..15.
capa:doc=Encode a byte list as a lowercase hex String, two chars per byte.

capa:doc=Rotate a 32-bit word right by `n` bits. `x` is assumed already
masked to 32 bits; `(x << (32 - n))` can reach 2^63 only if x had
high bits, so the whole expression is re-masked to 32 bits.
capa:doc=Logical right shift of an already-masked 32-bit word.

capa:doc=The 64 round constants (first 32 bits of the fractional parts of
the cube roots of the first 64 primes).

capa:doc=Read one 32-bit big-endian word from `block` starting at byte `i`.
Each byte is in 0..255; the top byte is masked to 8 bits before
the 24-bit shift so the product stays well under i64.
capa:doc=Build the 64-word message schedule for one 64-byte block.

capa:doc=One block of the compression function. Returns the updated state
(the eight working words added back into the incoming state).

capa:doc=SHA padding: append 0x80, then zeros, then the 64-bit big-endian
bit length, to a multiple of 64 bytes. Returns a fresh byte list.

capa:doc=Run the full hash over `message` starting from `iv`, returning the
eight 32-bit state words.
capa:doc=Serialize `count` state words as big-endian bytes (4 each).

capa:doc=SHA-256 digest of `message` as 32 raw bytes.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

capa:doc=SHA-256 digest of `message` as a 64-character lowercase hex String.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.
capa:doc=SHA-256 of a String's UTF-8 bytes, as lowercase hex.

capa:doc=SHA-224 digest of `message` as 28 raw bytes (the first seven
state words; the eighth is dropped).
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

capa:doc=SHA-224 digest of `message` as a 56-character lowercase hex String.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.
capa:doc=SHA-224 of a String's UTF-8 bytes, as lowercase hex.

capa:doc=Normalize a key to exactly 64 bytes: hash it if it is longer than
the block, then zero-pad to the block size.
capa:doc=XOR every byte of a 64-byte block with a constant pad byte.
capa:doc=Concatenate two byte lists into a fresh list.

capa:doc=HMAC-SHA256 of `message` under `key`, as 32 raw bytes.
BYTE CONTRACT: each element of `key` and `message` must be in
0..255. Out-of-range elements are silently masked to 8 bits
(`& 0xFF`: 256 -> 0, -1 -> 255), with no error, identically on
both backends. For text, prefer the `_utf8` wrapper or
`String.bytes()`, which are always in range.

capa:doc=HMAC-SHA256 of `message` under `key`, as lowercase hex.
BYTE CONTRACT: each element of `key` and `message` must be in
0..255; out-of-range elements are silently masked to 8 bits
(`& 0xFF`), with no error, identically on both backends.

capa:doc=HMAC-SHA256 over the UTF-8 bytes of a String key and message, as
lowercase hex.
